title: tarenna pumila
intro: Tarenna pumila, also known as the dwarf tarenna, is a small shrub or undershrub species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the Himalayas and parts of Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Tarenna pumila lie in its leaves, roots, and stems, which contain various bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts for medicinal use.
